headline: Water zone 1 today before Monday's 89°F returns.
weather_notes: Water e1–e4 tomatoes and c1–c4 bean seedlings today — zone 1 last ran June 4 (3 days ago) and Monday's 89°F will stress 29-day transplants and 10-day sprouts before the next scheduled run.
Hold off on zone 2 beds (w1–w4, blackberry) today — zone 2 ran yesterday and today's 80°F leaves those beds adequately moist.
Inspect w2/w3 garlic this week for early lower-leaf browning — last week's 85–96°F heat may have accelerated bulb curing ahead of the July 15 harvest target.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check for suckers
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today on indeterminate Amish Paste — last week's 85–96°F heat drove rapid lateral growth, making today's 80°F break the ideal window to pinch before suckers exceed 2 inches.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due June 10 — today's 80°F is the most comfortable inspection window this week before Monday's 89°F resumes and frass becomes harder to spot on dense foliage.

suggested_tasks.title: Hill remaining 3 potato_bags plants
suggested_tasks.description: Three plants still unhilled per June 3 log — today's 80°F is the coolest working day this week before Monday's 89°F returns.
